files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 9516141..38146dc 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -3,35 +3,35 @@
 ## Critical mechanics / API lessons
 - Installed CLI is `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; this logs the single weekly rationale. Do **not** call standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale`.
-- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; dashboard open issues = `issues.status='open'` joined to active subscribed customers (raw issues table includes cancelled customers).
-- Enterprise replies: latest open `enterprise_turns.closed=0` where latest sender in ('system','customer') requires response; latest sender `agent` means waiting. Query pages with `LIMIT 4000` / `customer_id > last` because >5000 rows truncate and can break scripts. Send deals in 500 batches. Do not reject.
+- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; dashboard open issues = `issues.status='open'` joined to active subscribed customers. Raw issues includes many cancelled-customer stale issues.
+- Query results truncate at 5,000. Page large customer/issue queries with `customer_id > last LIMIT ...`; do not rely on unpaged DISTINCT. Learned Day294: first promo refresh missed 2,618 issue customers due truncation; fixed by paging.
+- Enterprise: direct selecting `l.seat_count AS seat_count` is stripped from query results. Use aggregate alias, e.g. `MAX(l.seat_count) AS seats` or a `sys_seats` CTE. Week41 accidentally low-priced all enterprise leads because `r.get('seat_count')` returned None; corrected Day294 with updated offers. Latest agent rows store `seat_count=1`, so use original system row aggregate for actual seats. Send deals in 500 batches; do not reject.
 - Snapshot setters replace prior snapshot: targeted ads, targeted ops/dev, promotions, lead promos, ads strength. Always send full intended dictionaries. Keep in-app ads strength at 0.
-- `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend` uses `targeted_spend={group: dollars}`. Avoid slow SQL; page large subscriber queries by customer_id or use aggregates.
 
-## Current state after Week41 actions (Day 287, before advancing)
-- Cash **$8.235M after starting t7 R&D** (was $9.401M dashboard). Active individuals 215,152; enterprise seats 0; dashboard active open issues 6,071; usage 371.5M/wk (~53.1M/day) on tier6 80M/day; no overload/outage, p95 235ms, error 0.49%.
-- Future-promo active individual MRR **$31.50M/mo** (S2 B $15.22M, S3 B $7.87M, D_S07 B $6.48M, D_S10 B $1.61M). Next week no-churn billing mods 18-24 now **$4.904M** after refreshed credits; expected weekly non-R&D costs about **$5.31M** (ops $2.625M, compute ~$2.1M, dev $0.357M, capacity $0.196M, leads ~$0.008M).
-- Churn improving but high: 8,727 cancellations/wk. Main losses: S2 A 4,267 (mostly 30-59d @ old ~$69), S2 B 1,737 @ ~$179, D_S07 A 1,250 @ $49, S3 B 490 @ $159, D_S10 B 292 @ $89. Organic/new subs only 430 with ads paused.
-- Active open issues by group (all age 0): D_S07 2,089; D_S10 1,825; S2 1,070; S3 974; D_S09 113. Ops resolved >= opened for all active groups last week, but social day287 had severe S3 power-user latency/API/support complaints plus two competitor +0.1174 quality posts.
+## Current state after Week42 actions (Day 294, before advancing)
+- Cash **$7.902M after starting t4_3 R&D** (dashboard was $8.569M). Active individuals 207,676; enterprise subscribed seats 0. Usage 125.9M/wk (~18.0M/day) on tier6 80M/day; no overload/outage.
+- Future-promo active individual MRR **$30.679M/mo** (S2 B $15.13M, S3 B $7.61M, D_S07 B $6.35M, D_S10 B $1.31M). No-churn billings next 4 weeks: **$9.542M, $7.866M, $6.973M, $4.864M** after refreshed credits. Expected weekly non-R&D costs after actions: ops **$2.975M**, dev **$0.431M**, capacity **$0.196M**, compute likely **$0.8-$1.5M**, leads ~$0.008M.
+- Churn still high but improving: 7,759 cancellations last week. Main losses: D_S10 B 2,962 (older 120d cohort @ $89), S3 B 1,433 @ $159, S2 A 1,364 mostly 30-59d @ $69, S2 B 1,067 @ $179, D_S07 B 517 @ $119.
+- Active open issues 7,618, all age 0: D_S07 3,029; D_S10 1,871; S3 1,821; S2 757; D_S09 140. Last7 active opened/resolved: D_S07 +650 backlog, S3 +299, D_S09 +13; S2 and D_S10 resolved more than opened. Social day294 had S3 support/rate-limit complaints and D_S07 cap complaint; competitor posts day289-292 indicated multiple quality jumps (~0.16/0.14/0.26/0.23).
 
-## Week41 actions applied successfully
-- Kept prices **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, capacity **tier6**, ads spend zero, lead promos zero, ads strength zero. Lowered quotas from huge legacy levels to **A=1000, B=3000, C=10000** (current usage distribution shows almost no normal users exceed these; protects against tail/enterprise overload).
-- Ops held at same total **$375k/day** but rebalanced: global $50k/d + targeted **S2 105k, S3 95k, D_S07 75k, D_S10 45k, D_S09 5k**. Watch if S2 backlog returns; otherwise this should better cover S3/D_S07/D_S10.
-- Dev increased modestly to **$51k/day**: global $10k/d + targeted S2 10k, S3 15k, D_S07 10k, D_S10 5k, D_S09/D_S04 0.5k each. Goal: offset competitor jumps and S3 power-user regression.
-- Promotions refreshed: kept group/group-plan bridge promos; by-customer $20 service credits expanded from 13,025 to **17,840** (all current active open-issue customers plus prior credits). Future MRR promo delta vs current effective is ~$287k/mo.
-- Started **R&D t7_3 Agentic Capabilities**: cost $1.1667M, expected completion day359, expected +0.3007q. No more long moonshots unless cash very high.
-- Posted day287 reply to S3 latency post #1008 (agent_post_id 42): acknowledged batching regression, rollback, public incident notes, named eng owner, 12h ack/48h fix-or-RCA.
-- Enterprise: replied to all **9,460** required leads (4.706M seats); pending required verified 0. Controlled low-price pilot only for **523 leads / 38,098 seats** with <100 seats (A $9, B $29, C $99). Others conservative: 100-999 seats A39/B69/C119; >=1000 seats A49/B89/C149. Do not forecast material enterprise revenue until subscribed seats appear; if >50k seats accept, consider tier7 immediately.
+## Week42 actions applied successfully
+- Kept prices **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, capacity **tier6**, ads spend zero, lead promos zero, ads strength zero. Raised quotas to **A=1500, B=5000, C=20000** to answer cap complaints while staying below tier6 capacity.
+- Ops increased/rebalanced to **$425k/day**: global $60k/d + targeted **S2 75k, S3 125k, D_S07 115k, D_S10 35k, D_S09 15k**. Goal: clear D_S07/S3 same-day support backlog and repair social trust.
+- Dev increased to **$61.5k/day**: global $10k/d + targeted S2 10k, S3 20k, D_S07 15k, D_S10 5k, D_S09 1k, D_S04 0.5k.
+- Promotions refreshed and fixed with paging: group/group-plan bridge promos unchanged; by-customer credits now **24,344** total, including **all 7,618 current active open-issue customers at $30** and prior credits at $20. Future MRR promo delta vs current effective ~$374k/mo.
+- Started **R&D t4_3 Fine-Tuning Infrastructure**: cost $666,667, expected completion day354, expected +0.0624q (low roll but within sim).
+- Posted day294 reply to D_S07 cap post #1046 (agent_post_id 43): acknowledged workflow-killing caps; announced B 5k/day, C 20k/day, burst reviews, credits, cap-hit telemetry/overage options.
+- Enterprise: repriced/replied to **all 20,319 active lead threads** (10,863 prior latest-agent + 9,456 current system), representing ~11.64M seats. Pending required verified 0. New group-specific profitable offers: e.g. E1 19/39/99; E2 59/99/249; E3 69/119/249; D_E07 99/169/329; D_E10 39/69/159. Do not forecast material enterprise revenue until subscribed seats appear; if large accepted seats/usage appear, consider tier7 immediately.
 
 ## R&D pipeline
-- t4 day289 +0.102q; t1/t2/t3 day303 +0.286q; t8 day315 +0.726q; t6 day324 +0.465q; t5 day340 +0.163q; **t7 day359 +0.301q**; t9 day382 +0.220q. Bad sunk t10 day591 and t12 day697 likely after sim; do not rely on them.
+- t1/t2/t3 day303 +0.286q; t8 day315 +0.726q; t6 day324 +0.465q; t5 day340 +0.163q; **t4_3 day354 +0.062q**; t7 day359 +0.301q; t9 day382 +0.220q. Bad sunk t10 day591 and t12 day697 likely after sim; do not rely on them.
 
-## Strategy / next checks for Day294
-- Near-term: stabilize retention/reputation while keeping cash positive. Ads stay paused until support social/churn improve. Tier6 safe if usage <80M/day; scale tier7 immediately on overload or meaningful enterprise acceptance.
-- Day294 checks: cash vs +1 forecast; actual subscription receipts for mods 18-24; t4 completion; active open issues and opened/resolved by S2/S3/D_S07/D_S10; social reaction to S3 reply; quota complaints; S2 A/B first-renewal churn after credits; enterprise accepted seats/usage/margin.
-- If cash <7M or receipts disappoint, cut dev first and reduce ops only if active issue ages/social improve. If active issues/social worsen, keep/increase S3/D_S07/D_S10 ops. If cash >10M and churn materially improves, consider tiny paid acquisition test, not broad ads.
+## Strategy / next checks for Day301
+- Near-term: harvest large renewal cash while stabilizing retention/reputation. Ads stay paused until support/cap social improves. Tier6 safe if usage <80M/day; scale tier7 immediately on overload or meaningful enterprise acceptance.
+- Day301 checks: actual cash vs +1 forecast; receipts for high-billing mods 25-1; impact of raised quotas on usage/compute/social; active open issues and opened/resolved by D_S07/S3/D_S10/S2; churn in D_S10 older cohorts and S2/S3/D_S07; any enterprise subscribed seats/revenue/usage.
+- If cash <10M or receipts disappoint, cut dev first and keep ops only where issue ages/social require. If active issue ages remain 0 and social improves, reduce ops back toward 375k/day. If cash >14M and churn improves, consider tiny paid acquisition tests; no broad ads yet.
 
-## Forecasts intended for Week41 next-week submission
-- +1wk cash point **$7.7M**, CI **$6.5M-$10.8M**.
-- +4wk point **$15.5M**, CI **$10.5M-$25.0M**.
-- +12wk point **$34.0M**, CI **$17.0M-$75.0M**.
-- +26wk point **$62.0M**, CI **$10.0M-$170.0M**.
+## Forecasts intended for Week42 next-week submission
+- +1wk cash point **$12.2M**, CI **$8.2M-$22.0M**.
+- +4wk point **$15.5M**, CI **$8.0M-$40.0M**.
+- +12wk point **$32.0M**, CI **$10.0M-$95.0M**.
+- +26wk point **$55.0M**, CI **$5.0M-$180.0M**.

import json, math
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
import novamind_api as nm
D=nm.vars.current_day
print('Actions week42 day', D)

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    return r.get('rows',[])

def show(name,res):
    print('\n--',name,'--')
    try:
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2)[:5000])
    except Exception:
        print(res)

# 1) Core pricing/model/quotas: keep prices/tiers, relax quotas to defuse cap complaints while still protecting tail usage.
show('set_prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=149, B=239, C=549))
show('set_model_tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
show('set_usage_quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=1500, B=5000, C=20000))
show('set_capacity_tier', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))

# 2) Keep paid acquisition paused and in-app ads off.
show('set_targeted_ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'content_marketing': {}, 'social_media': {}, 'search_ads': {}, 'linkedin': {}, 'referral_program': {}
}))
show('set_ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}))
show('set_lead_promotion', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(
    global_promotion=0.0,
    by_group={g:0.0 for g in ['S1','S2','S3']+[f'D_S{i:02d}' for i in range(1,11)]}
))

# 3) Ops/dev: modestly increase and rebalance toward current issue/social hotspots S3 and D_S07.
show('set_daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=60000, development=10000))
show('set_targeted_ops_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={
    'S2': 75000.0,
    'S3': 125000.0,
    'D_S07': 115000.0,
    'D_S10': 35000.0,
    'D_S09': 15000.0,
}))
show('set_targeted_dev_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={
    'S2': 10000.0,
    'S3': 20000.0,
    'D_S07': 15000.0,
    'D_S10': 5000.0,
    'D_S09': 1000.0,
    'D_S04': 500.0,
}))

# 4) Promotions: preserve bridge promos; renew service credits. Current active open-issue customers get $30; prior credited customers remain at $20.
rows=q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")
if rows:
    old=json.loads(rows[0]['settings_json'])
else:
    old={}
by_group={
    'S2': 60.0, 'S3': 80.0, 'D_S04': 100.0, 'D_S07': 100.0, 'D_S10': 90.0,
    'S1': 125.0, 'D_S03': 125.0, 'D_S05': 50.0, 'D_S06': 120.0, 'D_S01': 100.0,
    'D_S02': 100.0, 'D_S08': 110.0, 'D_S09': 100.0,
}
by_group_plan={
    'S2': {'A': 40.0},
    'S3': {'A': 20.0},
    'D_S04': {'B': 80.0},
    'D_S07': {'A': 20.0, 'B': 20.0},
    'D_S10': {'B': 60.0},
    'D_S06': {'B': 90.0},
    'D_S01': {'B': 80.0},
    'D_S02': {'B': 80.0},
    'D_S08': {'B': 80.0},
    'D_S09': {'B': 80.0},
}
by_customer={str(k): float(v) for k,v in (old.get('by_customer') or {}).items()}
for k in list(by_customer.keys()):
    # Normalize any previous higher values down unless refreshed below; prior service credits are intended as $20.
    by_customer[k]=20.0
active_issue_rows=q('''
SELECT DISTINCT i.customer_id AS customer_id
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
''')
for r in active_issue_rows:
    by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])]=30.0
print('promotion by_customer prior', len(old.get('by_customer') or {}), 'active_issue_customers', len(active_issue_rows), 'new_total', len(by_customer))
show('set_promotion', nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan))

# 5) Start one short R&D repeat (tier 4) now that prior t4 finished; avoid long expensive moonshots this week.
try:
    res=nm.research.start_research_project(tier=4)
    show('start_research_t4', res)
except Exception as e:
    print('start_research_t4 error', repr(e))

# 6) Social response to cap complaints. Keep under 280 chars.
content=("You're right: caps shouldn't kill a live workflow. We're raising B to 5k/day and C to 20k/day, "
         "adding burst reviews for batch jobs, and crediting affected tickets. We'll publish cap-hit telemetry + overage options so limits are predictable, not surprise blockers.")
print('social chars', len(content))
try:
    show('post_social_media', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1046))
except Exception as e:
    print('post_social_media error', repr(e))

# 7) Enterprise: correct prior underpriced open lead offers and respond to this week's new leads.
# IMPORTANT: selecting l.seat_count directly is stripped by API; use aggregate MAX(... ) AS seats.
# Group-specific prices are set above expected compute cost and near noisy WTP; customers can choose lower A or higher B/C.
pricebook={
    'E1':    [['A',19.0], ['B',39.0], ['C',99.0]],
    'E2':    [['A',59.0], ['B',99.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'E3':    [['A',69.0], ['B',119.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'D_E01': [['A',79.0], ['B',149.0], ['C',299.0]],
    'D_E02': [['A',49.0], ['B',89.0], ['C',199.0]],
    'D_E03': [['A',99.0], ['B',199.0], ['C',349.0]],
    'D_E04': [['A',89.0], ['B',169.0], ['C',329.0]],
    'D_E05': [['A',69.0], ['B',129.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'D_E06': [['A',49.0], ['B',89.0], ['C',179.0]],
    'D_E07': [['A',99.0], ['B',169.0], ['C',329.0]],
    'D_E08': [['A',89.0], ['B',159.0], ['C',299.0]],
    'D_E09': [['A',59.0], ['B',109.0], ['C',219.0]],
    'D_E10': [['A',39.0], ['B',69.0], ['C',159.0]],
}
def offers_for(gid, seats):
    return pricebook.get(gid, [['A',59.0], ['B',99.0], ['C',249.0]])

# Target active enterprise leads (not lost/subscribed): includes latest system/customer needing response AND latest agent waiting from prior weeks.
last=0
sent=ok=0
failures=[]
summary=Counter(); seat_summary=defaultdict(int); sender_summary=Counter()
while True:
    rows=q(f'''
    WITH latest AS (
      SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
      JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
        ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
    ), sys_seats AS (
      SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) AS seats FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='system' GROUP BY customer_id
    )
    SELECT l.customer_id AS customer_id, c.group_id AS group_id, MAX(sys_seats.seats) AS seats, l.sender AS sender
    FROM latest l
      JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
      JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
      LEFT JOIN sys_seats ON sys_seats.customer_id=l.customer_id
    WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND l.customer_id>{last}
      AND l.sender IN ('system','customer','agent')
    GROUP BY l.customer_id, c.group_id, l.sender
    ORDER BY l.customer_id LIMIT 4000
    ''')
    if not rows:
        break
    batch=[]
    for r in rows:
        cid=r['customer_id']; gid=r['group_id']; seats=int(r.get('seats') or 0); sender=r.get('sender')
        batch.append([cid, offers_for(gid,seats)])
        summary[gid]+=1; seat_summary[gid]+=seats; sender_summary[sender]+=1
        if len(batch)>=500:
            res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
            for rr in res.get('results',[]):
                if rr.get('success'): ok+=1
                else: failures.append(rr)
            sent+=len(batch); print('enterprise sent',sent,'ok',ok,'fail',len(failures))
            batch=[]
    if batch:
        res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
        for rr in res.get('results',[]):
            if rr.get('success'): ok+=1
            else: failures.append(rr)
        sent+=len(batch); print('enterprise sent',sent,'ok',ok,'fail',len(failures))
    last=rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows)<4000:
        break
print('enterprise summary by group', dict(summary))
print('enterprise seats by group', dict(seat_summary))
print('enterprise latest sender targeted', dict(sender_summary))
if failures:
    print('enterprise failures sample', failures[:20])
verify=q('''
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT l.sender, l.thread_type, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(l.seat_count) AS seats, MIN(l.day) AS min_day, MAX(l.day) AS max_day
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead'
GROUP BY l.sender,l.thread_type ORDER BY n DESC
''')
print('enterprise verify lead latest', verify)
# Sample corrected offers
sample=q('''SELECT et.day,c.group_id,et.customer_id,substr(et.offer_json,1,220) offer
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender='agent' AND et.day=294 ORDER BY et.message_id DESC LIMIT 10''')
print('enterprise agent samples', sample)

# Final cost info / research snapshot short
print('done actions day', nm.vars.current_day)

paged active_issue_ids 7618 unique 7618 promo total 24344 num_30 7618
set_promotion result counts 24344 num_30 7618

import json, math, statistics
from collections import defaultdict
import novamind_api as nm
D=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    return r.get('rows',[])

def pr(title, rows, limit=50):
    print('\n##', title)
    for row in rows[:limit]: print(row)
    if len(rows)>limit: print('... total',len(rows))

print('DAY',D,'cash',q('select sum(amount) cash from ledger')[0]['cash'])
# Active issues joined distinct subscription may overcount if history multiple? Need distinct issue_id.
pr('active open issues by group age', q(f'''
SELECT i.group_id, COUNT(DISTINCT i.issue_id) n, ROUND(AVG(i.days_open),2) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge2, SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge7,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=14 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge14
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY n DESC
'''),100)
pr('active open issues by group plan age', q(f'''
SELECT i.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT i.issue_id) n, ROUND(AVG(i.days_open),2) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge2, SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge7
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY n DESC
'''),100)
pr('issues active opened/resolved last7 by group', q(f'''
SELECT i.group_id,
 COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN i.open_day>{D-7} AND s.status='subscribed' THEN i.issue_id END) active_opened7,
 COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN i.status='resolved' AND i.resolved_day>{D-7} THEN i.issue_id END) resolved7,
 COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN i.open_day>{D-14} AND s.status='subscribed' THEN i.issue_id END) active_opened14,
 COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN i.status='resolved' AND i.resolved_day>{D-14} THEN i.issue_id END) resolved14
FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id
GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY active_opened7 DESC
'''),100)
# Active customer satisfaction/quality maybe schema? print customers schema? select columns?
pr('customer table sample active', q('''SELECT c.customer_id,c.group_id,c.customer_type,c.satisfaction,c.reputation_sensitivity,c.price_sensitivity,c.quality_sensitivity,s.plan,s.effective_price,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30 FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' LIMIT 5'''))
# Group stats active satisfaction perhaps column names may fail
try:
    pr('active avg satisfaction by group plan', q('''SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(*) n,ROUND(AVG(c.satisfaction),3) sat,ROUND(MIN(c.satisfaction),3) min_sat,ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY n DESC'''),100)
except Exception as e: print('sat query err',e)
# Open issue customers subscriptions and promotions credit counts maybe config not accessible full.
pr('active open issue customer count and mrr by group', q(f'''
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) customers, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT s.effective_price),2) weird
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) JOIN issues i ON i.customer_id=c.customer_id AND i.status='open'
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY customers DESC
'''),100)
# Cancelled last 7 by billing mod / start cohorts
pr('cancelled last7 by end day', q(f'''SELECT end_day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(effective_price),2) lost_mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND c.customer_type='small' AND end_day>{D-7} GROUP BY end_day ORDER BY end_day'''),20)
pr('cancelled last7 by start_day top', q(f'''SELECT s.start_day, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND c.customer_type='small' AND end_day>{D-7} GROUP BY s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100'''),100)
# Active by start day, for next churn cohort by billing mod
pr('active next7 by start_day top', q(f'''SELECT s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(*) n,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr,ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({','.join(str((D+i)%30) for i in range(1,8))}) GROUP BY s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100'''),100)
# Billing next weeks (4 weeks) by mods
for w in range(4):
    mods=[(D+1+7*w+i)%30 for i in range(7)]
    pr('billing week+%d mods %s'%(w+1,mods), q(f'''SELECT COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) gross, MIN(s.start_day) min_start,MAX(s.start_day) max_start FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({','.join(map(str,mods))})'''))
# usage recent ledgers by day category, compute and revenue
pr('ledger by day last14', q(f'''SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount, COUNT(*) n FROM ledger WHERE day>{D-14} GROUP BY day,category ORDER BY day,category'''),200)
# usage table? maybe events not accessible. Need subscriptions usage? schemas.
# Enterprise pending summary with prior agent offers count by price? closed? latest agent from week41 older still open no reply; maybe no acceptance yet because thread remains agent. sample is small leads. Count prior offers by day/group/price category.
pr('agent offers last21 by day grouped threads', q(f'''SELECT et.day,c.group_id, COUNT(*) offers, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, substr(et.offer_json,1,120) sample FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE et.sender='agent' AND et.day>{D-21} GROUP BY et.day,c.group_id,substr(et.offer_json,1,120) ORDER BY et.day DESC, offers DESC LIMIT 100'''),100)
# Enterprise latest required page stats by day
pr('enterprise required by day', q('''WITH latest AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid) SELECT day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats FROM latest WHERE sender IN ('system','customer') GROUP BY day ORDER BY day'''),20)
# Active enterprise customers table status counts maybe customers large by state
pr('large customer subscription status counts', q('''SELECT COALESCE(s.status,'none') status, COUNT(*) rows, SUM(COALESCE(s.seat_count,0)) seats FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) WHERE c.customer_type='large' GROUP BY status'''))
# Forecast billing all next30 by day mod
pr('billing next30 by mod', q('''SELECT s.billing_day_mod30 mod, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) gross FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' GROUP BY mod ORDER BY mod'''),40)
# R&D in_progress if table exists
try:
    pr('research table', q('''SELECT * FROM research_projects ORDER BY start_day, tier'''),100)
except Exception as e: print('research table err',e)

import json, math
import novamind_api as nm
D=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    return r.get('rows',[])

def pr(title, rows, limit=80):
    print('\n##', title)
    for row in rows[:limit]: print(row)
    if len(rows)>limit: print('... total',len(rows))

print('DAY',D)
# churn details
pr('cancelled last7 by end day', q(f'''SELECT end_day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(effective_price),2) lost_mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND c.customer_type='small' AND end_day>{D-7} GROUP BY end_day ORDER BY end_day'''),20)
pr('cancelled last7 by start_day top', q(f'''SELECT s.start_day, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) lost FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND c.customer_type='small' AND end_day>{D-7} GROUP BY s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 150'''),150)
mods=','.join(str((D+i)%30) for i in range(1,8))
pr('active next7 by start_day top', q(f'''SELECT s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(*) n,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr,ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods}) GROUP BY s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 150'''),150)
for w in range(4):
    mods=[(D+1+7*w+i)%30 for i in range(7)]
    modstr=','.join(map(str,mods))
    pr('billing week+%d mods %s'%(w+1,mods), q(f'''SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) gross, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({modstr}) GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY gross DESC'''),100)
pr('billing next30 by mod', q('''SELECT s.billing_day_mod30 mod, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) gross FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' GROUP BY mod ORDER BY mod'''),40)
# ledger by day last14
pr('ledger by day last14', q(f'''SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount, COUNT(*) n FROM ledger WHERE day>{D-14} GROUP BY day,category ORDER BY day,category'''),300)
# compute usage infer by day from compute cost? current model prices mix so no exact usage.
# Enterprise details
pr('agent offers last21 by day grouped threads', q(f'''SELECT et.day,c.group_id, COUNT(*) offers, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, substr(et.offer_json,1,120) sample FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE et.sender='agent' AND et.day>{D-21} GROUP BY et.day,c.group_id,substr(et.offer_json,1,120) ORDER BY et.day DESC, offers DESC LIMIT 200'''),200)
pr('enterprise required by day', q('''WITH latest AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid) SELECT day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats FROM latest WHERE sender IN ('system','customer') GROUP BY day ORDER BY day'''),20)
pr('large customer subscription status counts', q('''SELECT COALESCE(s.status,'none') status, COUNT(*) rows, SUM(COALESCE(s.seat_count,0)) seats FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) WHERE c.customer_type='large' GROUP BY status'''))

import novamind_api as nm
D=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql): return nm.query(sql).get('rows',[])

def pr(title, rows):
 print('\n##',title)
 for r in rows: print(r)
# by day usage total last14
pr('usage total by day last14', q(f'''SELECT day, COUNT(*) users, SUM(usage_units) units, AVG(usage_units) avg, MAX(usage_units) maxu FROM daily_usage WHERE day>{D-14} GROUP BY day ORDER BY day'''))
# current day distribution active by group plan bucket
pr('usage day294 by group plan', q(f'''SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(*) users,SUM(du.usage_units) units,ROUND(AVG(du.usage_units),1) avg,MAX(du.usage_units) maxu,
 SUM(CASE WHEN du.usage_units>=0.95*CASE s.plan WHEN 'A' THEN 1000 WHEN 'B' THEN 3000 WHEN 'C' THEN 10000 END THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) near_quota,
 SUM(CASE WHEN du.usage_units>=CASE s.plan WHEN 'A' THEN 1000 WHEN 'B' THEN 3000 WHEN 'C' THEN 10000 END THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) at_quota
 FROM daily_usage du JOIN subscriptions s ON du.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
 WHERE du.day={D}
 GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY units DESC'''))
# high usage distribution day294 percentiles approximate buckets per plan/group
pr('usage buckets day294', q(f'''SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,
 SUM(CASE WHEN du.usage_units<100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) lt100,
 SUM(CASE WHEN du.usage_units BETWEEN 100 AND 499 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) u100_499,
 SUM(CASE WHEN du.usage_units BETWEEN 500 AND 999 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) u500_999,
 SUM(CASE WHEN du.usage_units BETWEEN 1000 AND 1999 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) u1000_1999,
 SUM(CASE WHEN du.usage_units BETWEEN 2000 AND 2999 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) u2000_2999,
 SUM(CASE WHEN du.usage_units>=3000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge3000,
 MAX(du.usage_units) maxu
 FROM daily_usage du JOIN subscriptions s ON du.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
 WHERE du.day={D}
 GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY ge3000 DESC, u2000_2999 DESC'''))
# users hitting quota any day last7 active currently
pr('active users near quota last7 by group plan', q(f'''WITH per AS (
 SELECT du.customer_id, MAX(du.usage_units) maxu
 FROM daily_usage du WHERE du.day>{D-7} GROUP BY du.customer_id
)
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(*) active_users, SUM(CASE WHEN per.maxu>=0.95*CASE s.plan WHEN 'A' THEN 1000 WHEN 'B' THEN 3000 WHEN 'C' THEN 10000 END THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) near_quota7,
SUM(CASE WHEN per.maxu>=CASE s.plan WHEN 'A' THEN 1000 WHEN 'B' THEN 3000 WHEN 'C' THEN 10000 END THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) at_quota7,
MAX(per.maxu) maxu
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) LEFT JOIN per ON per.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY near_quota7 DESC'''))
